title: गणेश गायत्री मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गणेश गायत्री मन्त्रः ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
एकदन्तं महाकायं लम्बोदरं गजाननम्।
विघ्ननाशकरं देवं हेरम्बं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥

सिन्दूरवर्णं द्विभुजं गणेशं
चतुर्भुजं पाशमङ्कुशधारिणम्।
मोदकं दन्तं च धारयन्तं
मूषकवाहनं भजामि नित्यम्॥

॥ गणेश गायत्री ॥
ॐ एकदन्ताय विद्महे
वक्रतुण्डाय धीमहि।
तन्नो दन्ती प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ अपर गणेश गायत्री ॥
ॐ तत्पुरुषाय विद्महे
महोत्कटाय धीमहि।
तन्नो दन्ती प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
य इमं गणेशगायत्रीं नित्यं पठति भक्तितः।
सर्वविघ्ना विनश्यन्ति सिद्धिर्भवति निश्चिता॥

॥ इति श्री गणेश गायत्री मन्त्रः सम्पूर्णः ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ganesha Gayatri Mantrah ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Ekadantam Mahakayam Lambodaram Gajananam.
Vighna-nashakaram Devam Herambam Pranamamy-aham.

Sindooravarnam Dvibhujam Ganesham
Chaturbhujam Pasha-ankusha-dharinam.
Modakam Dantam Cha Dharayantam
Mushakavahanam Bhajami Nityam.

|| Ganesha Gayatri ||
Om Ekadantaya Vidmahe
Vakratundaya Dheemahi.
Tanno Danti Prachodayat.

|| Apara Ganesha Gayatri ||
Om Tatpurushaya Vidmahe
Mahotkataya Dheemahi.
Tanno Danti Prachodayat.

|| Phalashruti ||
Ya Imam Ganesha-gayatrim Nityam Pathati Bhaktitah.
Sarva-vighna Vinashyanti Siddhir-bhavati Nishchita.

|| Iti Shri Ganesha Gayatri Mantrah Sampurnah ||
hindiMeaning:
ध्यान: एकदन्त, महाकाय, लम्बोदर, गजानन, विघ्ननाशक देव हेरम्ब (गणेश) को मैं प्रणाम करता हूँ। सिन्दूरवर्ण, चतुर्भुज, पाश-अंकुश-मोदक-दन्त धारी, मूषकवाहन गणेश को नित्य भजता हूँ।

गायत्री: हम एकदन्त (गणेश) को जानते हैं, वक्रतुण्ड का ध्यान करते हैं। वे दन्ती (गणेश) हमारी बुद्धि को प्रेरित करें।

अपर गायत्री: हम महोत्कट को जानते हैं, उनका ध्यान करते हैं। वे दन्ती हमें प्रेरित करें।

जो भक्तिपूर्वक इस गणेश गायत्री का नित्य पाठ करता है, उसके सर्व विघ्न नष्ट होते हैं और निश्चित सिद्धि प्राप्त होती है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीगणेशगायत्रीमन्त्रस्य गणक ऋषिः। गायत्री छन्दः। श्री गणेश देवता। ॐ गं बीजम्। विघ्ननाशन शक्तिः। सर्वसिद्धि प्राप्ति कीलकम्। श्रीगणेशप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

Ideal to chant before beginning any puja or ritual
Enhances learning ability and academic performance
Powerful mantra for Ganesha Chaturthi and Wednesdays
description: The Ganesha Gayatri Mantra, known in Sanskrit as गणेश गायत्री मन्त्र, is a powerful Vedic meditation mantra in praise of Lord Ganesha. Rooted in Ganapati Atharvashirsha / Rig Veda tradition, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to enhance learning ability and academic performance as well as grant wisdom, intelligence, and clarity of thought. Additionally, it is known to bestow success and Siddhi with certainty. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Ganesh Chaturthi. The text contains 4 verses and takes around 3 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Ganesha Gayatri Mantra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ganesha Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganesha Gayatri Mantra (गणेश गायत्री मन्त्र) is the classical Vedic-style Gayatri composed in honour of Lord Ganesha — Ganapati — the deity of buddhi, vighna-nashana and primary worship in all Hindu rituals. It is one of the Pancha Devata Gayatris (Brahma Gayatri, Vishnu Gayatri, Shiva-Rudra Gayatri, Ganesha Gayatri, Surya Gayatri) drawn from Vedic and Puranic sources, and is preserved in classical recitation tradition with two principal versions: (1) Tat-Purushaya Vidmahe Vakratundaya Dhimahi, Tan No Dantih Prachodayat (the most widely chanted form, modeled on the Vedic Gayatri pattern); (2) Ekadantaya Vidmahe Vakratundaya Dhimahi, Tan No Dantih Prachodayat (substituting Ekadanta for Tat-Purusha). It is recited daily by Ganesha devotees, before all puja and study, and especially during Ganesh Chaturthi parayanas. (Sources: Ganapati Atharvashirsha tradition; Pancha Devata Gayatri compilation.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the Ganesha Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The classical Ganesha Gayatri — Tat-Purushaya Vidmahe Vakratundaya Dhimahi, Tan No Dantih Prachodayat — follows the structure of the original Savitri Gayatri of Rigveda 3.62.10. The meaning: Tat-Purushaya vidmahe — 'May we know that Supreme Person' (Purusha here being Ganesha as the cosmic Person, drawing on the Yajurveda Tat-Purusha mantra of Sri Rudram); Vakratundaya dhimahi — 'May we meditate upon him of the curved trunk' (Vakratunda being one of Ganesha's principal names from the Ganapati Atharvashirsha); Tan no Dantih prachodayat — 'May that One-Tusked one (Dantih = Ekadanta) inspire and enlighten our intellect.' Together the mantra prays: 'May we know the Supreme Person, may we meditate on the curved-trunk Ganesha, and may that one-tusked Lord enlighten our buddhi.' It is therefore both a praise and a request for buddhi-prerana — divine inspiration of intellect. (Sources: Ganapati Atharvashirsha; classical Vedic Gayatri tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Ganesha Gayatri related to the original Savitri Gayatri of the Rigveda?
seoFaqs.answer: The original Savitri Gayatri — Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah, Tat Savitur Varenyam Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi, Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat (Rigveda 3.62.10) — is the supreme mantra of the Vedic tradition, addressed to Savitri-Surya as the source of all illumination. The Pancha Devata Gayatris (including the Ganesha Gayatri) are later compositions that adopt the structural pattern of the Savitri Gayatri but substitute the deity-aspect: (1) the vidmahe clause names the cosmic identity of the deity (Tat-Purusha for Ganesha, Mahadevyai for Devi, Narayanaya for Vishnu, Rudraya for Shiva); (2) the dhimahi clause names the form to meditate upon (Vakratunda, Mahalakshmyai, Vasudevaya, Mahadevaya); (3) the prachodayat clause names the bestowing form (Dantih for Ganesha, Devi for Shakti, Vishnu for Vaishnavas, Rudra for Shaivas). The Ganesha Gayatri thus carries all the cosmic mantra-shakti of the original Savitri Gayatri, channeled through Ganesha as the deity of buddhi. (Sources: Rigveda 3.62.10; classical Pancha Devata Gayatri compilation.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Ganesha Gayatri be chanted?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ganesha Gayatri is most powerfully chanted at Brahma muhurta in the early morning, after a bath, facing east, on a clean asana, with concentration on the meaning of each word. It is also recited at sandhya times (sunrise, noon, sunset) along with the regular Savitri Gayatri. Special occasions for parayana include: every Chaturthi tithi (Sankashti and Vinayaka Chaturthis), Tuesdays and Fridays, Bhadrapada Shukla Chaturthi (Ganesh Chaturthi — when 1008 or 10000 recitations are traditional), Magha Shukla Chaturthi (Ganesh Jayanti), every Ganesha temple visit, before the start of any new venture, before examinations and study, and at the beginning of every puja or katha (Ganesha being prathamya pujya). The standard japa-counts are 11, 21, 28, 108, 1008. The recitation should follow the Vedic svara (with udatta, anudatta and svarita) for maximum mantra-shakti. (Sources: classical Vedic recitation paddhati; Ganesha worship tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who can chant the Ganesha Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: Unlike the original Savitri Gayatri which in classical Smriti tradition is restricted to dvija (twice-born, after upanayana initiation) — though many modern teachers like Swami Dayananda and Swami Chinmayananda have democratised it to all sincere seekers — the Ganesha Gayatri (and the other Pancha Devata Gayatris) is universally chanted across all varnas, ashramas, ages and genders without restriction. It is especially recommended for: students at all levels seeking buddhi, concentration and academic success; children for buddhi-vikasa from a young age; women devotees of Ganesha; persons starting any new venture; those facing obstacles in business, marriage or career; and devotees of any sampradaya since Ganesha is universally the prathamya pujya (first-worshipped) deity. The mantra requires no diksha and no formal initiation, though chanting it for the first time before a Ganesha murti and offering durva and a modak is traditionally recommended. (Sources: classical Smriti tradition; modern Vedanta teachers' guidance.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Ganesha Gayatri bestow according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: Classical tradition and the Ganapati Atharvashirsha attribute the following benefits to regular chanting of the Ganesha Gayatri: profound buddhi-vikasa (development of intellect), enhanced memory, concentration and grasping power; success in all studies, examinations, competitive tests and intellectual pursuits; removal of all obstacles (vighna-nashana) in any work undertaken — Ganesha being Vighnaharta; pacification of Ketu graha (Ganesha's adhipatya) in the natal chart; cure of nervous disorders, anxiety, headaches, learning disabilities and concentration deficits; cultivation of vivek (discrimination) and dhi-shakti (power of comprehension); the special phala of mantra-prerana — the mantra itself prays for prachodayat, divine inspiration; protection of children and their educational welfare; success of new ventures, business and marriage; and ultimately the supreme phala of jnana — true knowledge through the awakened buddhi, leading to liberation. (Sources: Ganapati Atharvashirsha phalashruti; classical Pancha Devata Gayatri tradition.)
